name: sherwood

seo.title: Sherwood AR Neighborhood Guide | Metro Little Rock
seo.description: Sherwood is an affordable northeast suburb of Little Rock with solid schools, established neighborhoods, and quick access to I-40 and downtown North Little Rock.

hero_image.alt: Suburban neighborhood streets in Sherwood, AR

intro: Sherwood is a northeast suburban city of about 32,000 residents that offers Little Rock metro living at some of the most accessible price points in Pulaski County. Situated along I-40 northeast of North Little Rock, it delivers solid suburban fundamentals — good schools, safe neighborhoods, mature trees, and convenient freeway access — at prices that consistently outcompete the south side of the river.
real_estate_overview: Sherwood's housing market is defined by attainability. The stock is primarily 1970s–2000s construction: brick ranches, Cape Cods, and traditional two-stories on modest lots in well-maintained subdivisions. The $175,000–$280,000 range gets a buyer a solid 3-4 bedroom home in most neighborhoods. A small supply of newer construction in the $290,000–$380,000 range exists in subdivisions near the Sherwood/North Little Rock border. The market has been consistently appreciating as buyers are priced out of NLR and downtown and look east. Investor activity is modest compared to intown neighborhoods.

schools_section.overview: Sherwood is served by the Pulaski County Special School District. The Sylvan Hills cluster — Sylvan Hills High School, Middle School, and associated elementary schools — has a strong reputation and is a primary driver of family relocations to Sherwood.

highlights.name: Sylvan Hills High School
highlights.note: Well-regarded PCSSD high school with strong athletics, AP programs, and consistently above-average test scores.

highlights.name: Sylvan Hills Middle School
highlights.note: K-8 feeder for the high school with a strong band program and active parent organization.

highlights.name: Oakbrooke Elementary
highlights.note: Popular K-5 in the northeast Sherwood area with strong parent involvement.

lifestyle_section.overview: Sherwood is a quiet, residential suburb. The lifestyle centers on neighborhood parks, youth athletics, and the convenience of I-40 access. The commercial corridor along Highway 107 and Kiehl Avenue handles most daily needs. For entertainment and dining beyond the basics, residents drive 15–20 minutes to Argenta/NLR or downtown.
lifestyle_section.dining: The Kiehl Avenue and Highway 107 corridors have a functional mix of casual restaurants and fast food. For destination dining, residents head to North Little Rock's Argenta district or downtown Little Rock.
lifestyle_section.outdoor: Burns Park in NLR, one of the largest municipally owned parks in the U.S. at 1,700+ acres, is 10 minutes from Sherwood and serves as the primary outdoor recreation hub. Sherwood itself has neighborhood parks and a trail system in development.
lifestyle_section.culture: Sherwood is a residential community — cultural activity is accessed by driving to NLR or Little Rock. The city has a community center and runs youth sports leagues.
lifestyle_section.family: Sherwood is a reliable family suburb. Good schools, safe streets, affordable prices, and proximity to Burns Park make it a practical choice for families who need the most house for their budget.

commute_section.overview: Sherwood's I-40 access makes it competitive for commutes across the metro's north side. The drive to downtown LR is about 25 minutes; to Cabot or Jacksonville it's 20–25 minutes east.

key_routes.notes: I-40 E; light traffic most of the day.
I-40 W to I-440 — one of the easier airport commutes.

buyer_profile.typical_buyer: A family priced out of NLR who wants to stay in Pulaski County with good schools, or a first-time buyer looking for the most house in the metro for $200,000–$260,000.

faqs.question: Is Sherwood in Pulaski County?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sherwood is an incorporated city in Pulaski County. It's served by the PCSSD school district and Pulaski County government, just like Maumelle and NLR.

faqs.question: How does Sherwood compare to Jacksonville for buyers?
faqs.answer: Sherwood is generally more expensive than Jacksonville (median $215K vs. $165K) with slightly better schools and more retail/dining nearby. Jacksonville has a stronger military community (Little Rock AFB) and very affordable entry points. Both are practical choices for value-focused buyers.
